messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy. The context pack is CONTEXT ONLY: do not extract candidates from it and do not use it as the evidence source. Extract candidates only from FOCUS SECTION TEXT, and every evidence_quote must be copied from FOCUS SECTION TEXT. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.4. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 40 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.
[redacted: ASHRAE section text omitted from artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are tasked to extract knowledge from a specific focus section: 5.4. The focus sections list is "5.4". We need to extract candidates whose primary section_id is exactly 5.4 or starts with 5.4. (like 5.4.1, 5.4.2, etc.). The provided FOCUS SECTION TEXT is the content under [[requested_section=5.4 page_range=71-71 title=5.4 Zone Groups]] and it includes subsections 5.4.1 through 5.4.6.1. So we should extract knowledge from that text.

Allowed knowledge types: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance.

We need to find operational knowledge (control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, application rules). The focus section is mostly about zone groups and their operating modes, testing overrides, requirements. Let's parse the text carefully.

The text begins:

5.4 Zone Groups
5.4.1. Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Group s composed of a collection of one or more zones served by a single air handler. See Section 3.1.3 for Zone Group assignments.

This is a requirement: Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Groups... This is an operational sequence? It's more of a design requirement. But it's a rule: the system must be divided into zone groups, each group served by one AHU. That could be a parameter_spec or application_guidance. Let's see: application_guidance is about how to apply the sequences. So this is guidance on how to structure zone groups. We'll capture it.

5.4.2. Each Zone Group shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes from other Zone Groups. (Then there's a note about user point of view and schedule superimposition, but that's informative? The requirement is "shall be capable". So it's a required capability.

5.4.3. All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone-group operating mode as defined in Section 5.4.6. If one zone in a Zone Group is placed in any zone-group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode. Occupied-standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode, and shall not be considered a zone-group operating mode.

This is an operational sequence about zone group mode propagation.

5.4.4. A Zone Group may be in only one mode at a given time.

5.4.5. For each Zone Group, provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones served by the Zone Group. Provide a separate software switch for each of the zone-level override switches listed under “Testing and Commissioning Overrides” in terminal unit sequences. When the value of a Zone Group’s override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the Zone Group shall change to the same value. Subsequently, the zone-level override switch may be changed to a different value. The value of the zone-level switch has no effect on the value of the zone-group switch, and the value of the zone-group switch only affects the zone-level switches when the zone-group switch is changed.

This is about commissioning override switches, with behavior described. It could be a commissioning_step or parameter_spec. It specifies a software switch and its behavior.

5.4.6. Zone-Group Operating Modes. Each Zone Group shall have the modes shown in the following subsections. The modes are to enable different setpoints and ventilation requirements based on operating schedule, occupancy status, and deviation from current setpoint. See ASHRAE Guideline 13 for best practices in locating zone-group operating mode programming logic based on network architecture.

5.4.6.1. Occupied Mode. A Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode when any of the following is true: a. The time of day is between the Zone Group’s scheduled occupied start and stop times. b. The schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system.

This defines the condition for Occupied Mode. It's an operational_sequence (the rule for mode determination).

We need to extract candidates. The instruction: "Extract only knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.4." So all these subsections qualify. We'll extract them.

Let's produce candidate objects with knowledge_type, title, section_id, section_title, equipment_scope, summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, commissioning_check, evidence_quote, page_hint, confidence.

We'll need to reference the page numbers: from the anchor, it's page 71. So page_hint = 71.

We need evidence_quote exactly from the text. We'll extract each distinct requirement or operational rule.

Candidate list:

1. From 5.4.1: requirement that each system is broken into zone groups. Could be application_guidance? It's a rule for system configuration. Parameter_spec? No, it's about the system structure. I'll classify as operational_sequence because it's a rule for how the system must be organized. But "operational_sequence" typically includes control sequences. This is more like design requirement. Yet "application_guidance" is for guidance on applying sequences. So maybe "application_guidance". The description: "application_guidance: Guidance on how to apply the sequences to specific applications or equipment." That fits. Or could be "parameter_spec" if it's specifying a parameter. It says "Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Groups". So it's a requirement. I'll go with application_guidance.

Evidence: "Each system shall be broken into separate Zone Group s composed of a collection of one or more zones served by a single air handler. See Section 3.1.3 for Zone Group assignments."

2. 5.4.2: "Each Zone Group shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes from other Zone Groups." This is a capability requirement. Could be parameter_spec? It's specifying a requirement for Zone Groups. I'll treat as parameter_spec, but it's more of a system behavior requirement. Possibly operational_sequence. I'll go with operational_sequence as it's about how zone groups shall operate (capable of separate schedules/modes). Or application_guidance. The line: "Note that, from the user’s point of view..." is informative, not requirement. The requirement is the shall statement. I'll capture that.

Evidence: "Each Zone Group shall be capable of having separate occupancy schedules and operating modes from other Zone Group s."

3. 5.4.3: Two parts: (a) All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone-group operating mode. (b) If one zone is placed in any mode other than Unoccupied, all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode. (c) Occupied-standby mode is not considered a zone-group operating mode.

We could combine the first two into one candidate? The requirement is that all zones share the mode, and the trigger is one zone being placed in non-unoccupied mode. The evidence quote should include the entire sentence: "All zones in each Zone Group shall be in the same zone -group operating mode as defined in Section 5.4.6. If one zone in a Zone Group is placed in any zone -group operating mode other than Unoccupied Mode (due to override, sequence logic, or scheduled occupancy), all zones in that Zone Group shall enter that mode."

That's one operational_sequence. Then the note about occupied-standby is separate: "Occupied -standby mode applies to individual zones, is considered a zonal subset of Occupied Mode, and shall not be considered a zone-group operating mode." That's another operational_sequence clarifying the definition. Could be a single candidate? Better to separate. I'll extract the first as an operational_sequence with trigger and behavior. The second as application_guidance or parameter_spec? It's a rule about what is excluded. I'll keep it as operational_sequence: "Occupied-standby mode shall not be considered a zone-group operating mode."

4. 5.4.4: "A Zone Group may be in only one mode at a given time." This is a rule. operational_sequence.

5. 5.4.5: This describes commissioning override switches. It includes providing switches, behavior when zone-group switch is changed, and independence. Extract as commissioning_step and operational_sequence. The main requirement: "For each Zone Group, provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones served by the Zone Group. Provide a separate software switch for each of the zone -level override switches listed under “Testing and Commissioning Overrides” in terminal unit sequences." Then: "When the value of a Zone Group’s override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the Zone Group shall change to the same value. Subsequently, the zone-level override switch may be changed to a different value. The value of the zone-level switch has no effect on the value of the zone-group switch, and the value of the zone-group switch only affects the zone-level switches when the zone-group switch is changed."

That's a detailed behavioral rule. We can extract as commissioning_step (the provision of switches) and operational_sequence (the behavior). But since they are combined, I'll create one commissioning_step: "provide testing/commissioning software switches" with evidence? However, the knowledge_type commissioning_step expects a check or verification action. This is more of a design specification for commissioning overrides. I could use "parameter_spec" for the requirement to provide switches? Better as "commissioning_step" since it's about commissioning overrides. The note says "The testing and commissioning overrides will be specified ...". So it's indeed a commissioning feature. I'll set knowledge_type to commissioning_step, and include the behavior as part of it? Or separate. Let's extract the whole behavior as one operational_sequence. I'll do two: one for the provision (which is a requirement, can be parameter_spec), and one for the behavior (operational_sequence). But the text includes both. The evidence quote will be the whole paragraph. I'll create two candidates to capture the distinct knowledge. The behavioral rule: "When the value of a Zone Group’s override switch is changed, the corresponding override switch for every zone in the Zone Group shall change to the same value. Subsequently, the zone-level override switch may be changed to a different value. The value of the zone-level switch has no effect on the value of the zone-group switch, and the value of the zone-group switch only affects the zone-level switches when the zone-group switch is changed."

Additionally, the first sentence: "For each Zone Group, provide a set of testing/commissioning software switches that override all zones served by the Zone Group. Provide a separate software switch for each of the zone -level override switches listed under “Testing and Commissioning Overrides” in terminal unit sequences." That's a design requirement for commissioning. I'll extract as commissioning_step.

6. 5.4.6: Each Zone Group shall have the modes shown in subsections. This is a requirement. Could be parameter_spec or operational_sequence. But the actual mode definitions are in 5.4.6.1. So we might not need to extract the heading alone. We'll extract the mode definition in 5.4.6.1.

7. 5.4.6.1: Occupied Mode condition. "A Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode when any of the following is true: a. The time of day is between the Zone Group’s scheduled occupied start and stop times. b. The schedules have been overridden by the occupant override system." This is operational_sequence (mode determination rule).

Also the note from 5.4.6: "The modes presented in this section are to enable different setpoints and ventilation requirements to be applied to Zone Groups based on their operating schedule, occupancy status, and deviation from current setpoint." This could be application_guidance. But might be too general. I'll skip.

Now, confidence: I'll assign high confidence (0.9) to these, as they are clear requirements.

We should output up to 40 candidates. We have about 6-7 distinct items. Let's compile into JSON list.

I need to include configurable_values, trigger_condition, required_behavior, fault_condition, commissioning_check as applicable. For operational_sequence, I should provide summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values if any. For commissioning_step, I need commissioning_check. For parameter_spec, configurable_values and maybe setpoint. For application_guidance, a summary.
